name: Lemon Curd-Coconut Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup butter or margarine, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease two 9-inch round layer-cake pans; dust with flour and tap out excess.
3. Prepare the cake batter:
a. Sift together the cake flour, baking powder, and salt onto a sheet of waxed paper.
b. Combine milk, cold water, and vanilla in a small bowl.
c. Beat egg whites in a clean bowl until frothy.
d. Gradually beat in 1/2 cup of sugar until soft peaks form.
e. Beat butter and remaining sugar in a large bowl on medium-high speed until fluffy and light-colored, about 2 minutes.
f. Add flour mixture alternately with milk mixture, beating on low speed after each addition.
g. Fold in beaten egg whites, half at a time.
4. Divide batter evenly between the prepared pans.
5. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until edges start to pull away from the sides of the pans. Cool cakes in the pans on racks for 10 minutes before turning them out onto wire racks to cool completely.
6. Prepare the frosting:
a. Combine sugar, cream of tartar, salt, egg whites, and cold water in the top of a double boiler.
b. Beat until thick, shiny, and forms stiff peaks, about 6 minutes over simmering water.
c. Remove from heat and let cool.
d. Add vanilla and beat until smooth.
7. Place one cake layer on a serving plate. Spread with lemon curd.
8. Top with second layer. Spread the top and sides with frosting.
9. Press shredded unsweetened coconut onto the top and sides.
